1→# CLAUDE.md
     2→
     3→## Project Overview
     4→
     5→Oscar Medical Guidelines scraper + structured criteria tree explorer. The system:
     6→1. Discovers and downloads all medical guideline PDFs from https://www.hioscar.com/clinical-guidelines/medical
     7→2. Uses an LLM (OpenAI) to structure at least 10 guidelines' **initial** medical necessity criteria into JSON decision trees
     8→3. Persists scraped policy metadata and structured trees in a database
     9→4. Provides a UI to browse policies and navigate/render criteria trees
    10→
    11→## Architecture (confirmed by research)
    12→
    13→- **Backend**: FastAPI + SQLAlchemy Core (raw SQL via `text()`, NOT ORM) + SQLite (WAL mode)
    14→- **Frontend**: Vite + React + TypeScript + React Router + Tailwind CSS
    15→- **Tree rendering**: Custom recursive component (no third-party library)
    16→- **DB**: SQLite at `data/app.db`, auto-migrated on startup from `backend/sql/sqlite_schema.sql`
    17→
    18→## Key Files
    19→
    20→- `oscar.json` — Target JSON structure. Recursive: `{title, insurance_name, rules: {rule_id, rule_text, operator?, rules?[]}}`
    21→- `backend/main.py` — FastAPI app with 4 endpoints (policies list, detail, tree, stats) using raw SQL with CTEs
    22→- `backend/sql/sqlite_schema.sql` — Schema DDL (3 tables + indexes)
    23→- `backend/migrate.py` — Runs schema SQL at startup
    24→- `backend/validator.py` — Pydantic recursive validation of LLM output
    25→- `backend/scraper.py` — B2+B3: Discovery + PDF resolver + downloader (requests + BS4 + __NEXT_DATA__)
    26→- `backend/extractor.py` — B5+B5.5: PDF text extraction (PyMuPDF) + initial section extraction
    27→- `backend/structurer.py` — B6: LLM structuring pipeline (OpenAI GPT-4o + JSON mode)
    28→- `backend/interfaces.py` — Protocol contracts for each pipeline bead
    29→- `frontend/src/components/TreeViewer.tsx` — Recursive tree with expand/collapse, AND/OR badges
    30→- `AGENTS.md` — Agent instructions for bd workflow
    31→- `BEADS.md` — Legacy reference (details per bead). Live tracking now in `bd` database
    32→
    33→## Database Patterns
    34→
    35→- SQLite pragmas: `foreign_keys=ON`, `journal_mode=WAL`, `busy_timeout=5000`
    36→- `check_same_thread=False` for web server threading
    37→- Policy UPSERT: `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT(pdf_url) DO UPDATE`
    38→- Structured UPSERT: `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT(policy_id) DO UPDATE`
    39→- Latest download: CTE with `ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY policy_id ORDER BY downloaded_at DESC)`
    40→- JSON stored as TEXT, parsed with `_parse_json_maybe()`
    41→
    42→## API Endpoints
    43→
    44→- `GET /api/policies` — List all with download_status + has_structured_tree
    45→- `GET /api/policies/:id` — Detail with latest download + structured data
    46→- `GET /api/policies/:id/tree` — Raw structured JSON tree
    47→- `GET /api/stats` — Counts: total_policies, downloaded, failed, structured
    48→- `POST /api/run-scraper` — Trigger B2+B3 pipeline (discover + resolve + download all PDFs)
    49→- `POST /api/run-structurer` — Trigger B5+B6 pipeline (extract text + LLM structuring, limit=10)
    50→
    51→## Scraping Architecture (from ChatGPT Deep Research)
    52→
    53→- **Listing page** (`/clinical-guidelines/medical`): Single HTML page, no pagination
    54→- **PDF links are NOT direct**: `<a>` tags with text "PDF" → **intermediate pages** (e.g., `/medical/cg013v11`)
    55→- **Intermediate pages**: Next.js SSR — parse `<script id="__NEXT_DATA__">` JSON for real PDF URL
    56→- **Real PDF host**: `assets.ctfassets.net` (Contentful CDN)
    57→- **Resolution**: Recursively walk `__NEXT_DATA__` JSON for `ctfassets.net` URLs, prefer `.pdf` suffix
    58→- **URL prefixes vary**: `/medical/cg*`, `/pharmacy/pg*`, `/pharmacy/cg*`, `/medical/adopted/*` — follow href
    59→- **Dedup by intermediate URL** (listing has duplicates)
    60→- **Filter**: Only "PDF" link text, skip "LINK" entries
    61→- **Complete scraper skeleton**: `research/1chatgpt.md`
    62→
    63→## Critical Constraints
    64→
    65→- **Initial criteria only**: Extract only the Initial tree (not Continuation/Repair/Revision)
    66→- **Initial heading patterns**: "Medical Necessity Criteria for Initial Authorization", "Clinical Indications" (first block)
    67→- **Exclude patterns**: "Reauthorization", "Continued Care", "Continuation of Services"
    68→- **Idempotent reruns**: `pdf_url` UNIQUE + UPSERT patterns
    69→- **Polite scraping**: 0.5s delay + jitter, retry 3x with backoff, browser-like User-Agent
    70→- **Schema validation**: Pydantic recursive model validation before DB insert
    71→
    72→## Issue Tracking — bd (beads)
    73→
    74→This project uses **bd** (Steve Yegge's Beads) for ALL task tracking. Backed by Dolt (version-controlled SQL). See AGENTS.md for full agent workflow.
    75→
    76→```bash
    77→bd ready              # Find unblocked work — START HERE every session
    78→bd show <id>          # View issue details + description
    79→bd update <id> --claim  # Claim work atomically
    80→bd close <id> --reason "Done"  # Complete work
    81→bd list               # List all open issues
    82→bd graph --all        # Visualize dependency chain
    83→bd create "title" -d "context" -t feature -p 1  # Create new issue
    84→```
    85→
    86→**Rules:**
    87→- Do NOT use markdown TODOs, TASKS.md, or competing task files — bd is the single source of truth
    88→- Always `bd ready` before starting work to find unblocked issues
    89→- Create discovered issues with `bd create "title" -d "context" --deps discovered-from:<parent-id>`
    90→- Include bead ID in commit messages: `git commit -m "Add scraper (full-stack-feb-mw1)"`
    91→- When closing a bead, update bd BEFORE merging to main: `bd close <id> --reason "Completed"`
    92→- Never use `bd edit` (interactive) — use `bd update <id> --description "text"` instead
    93→
    94→## Git Workflow (parallel-safe, beads-integrated)
    95→
    96→**Multiple Claude sessions may run simultaneously on different branches.**
    97→
    98→### Session Start
    99→```bash
   100→cd /home/gian/Projects/full-stack-feb/full-stack-feb
   101→git checkout main && git pull origin main
   102→git checkout -b <descriptive-branch-name>   # e.g. scraper, llm-pipeline, ui-tree
   103→bd ready                                     # Find what to work on
   104→bd update <id> --claim                       # Claim the bead
   105→```
   106→
   107→### While Working
   108→- **Commit + push after EVERY meaningful change** (new file, function complete, bug fix)
   109→- `git add <files> && git commit -m "description (bead-id)" && git push origin <branch>`
   110→- Aim for a commit+push every 5-10 minutes — unpushed work is LOST if session dies
   111→
   112→### Before Merge to Main (MANDATORY)
   113→```bash
   114→# TypeScript type-check MUST pass before any merge to main
   115→npx tsc --noEmit
   116→```
   117→If `tsc` fails, fix ALL type errors on your feature branch before proceeding.
   118→
   119→### Merge to Main (end of session)
   120→```bash
   121→git add -A && git commit -m "final changes (bead-id)" && git push origin <branch>
   122→bd close <id> --reason "Completed"           # Close the bead FIRST
   123→git checkout main && git pull origin main     # Get latest
   124→git checkout <branch>                         # Back to feature branch
   125→git merge main                               # Merge main INTO branch (resolve conflicts here)
   126→npx tsc --noEmit                             # Type-check MUST pass
   127→git checkout main && git merge <branch>       # Fast-forward merge to main
   128→git push origin main
   129→git push origin --delete <branch> && git branch -d <branch>
   130→```
   131→
   132→### Critical Rules
   133→- NEVER `git push --force` to main
   134→- NEVER merge to main without `npx tsc --noEmit` passing first
   135→- ALWAYS resolve conflicts on the feature branch, not on main
   136→- If `git push origin main` fails (another session pushed), repeat the merge steps
   137→- Before merging, check `git log origin/main --oneline -5` for other sessions' work
   138→
   139→## Commands
   140→
   141→```bash
   142→# Backend
   143→cd backend && pip install -r requirements.txt
   144→uvicorn backend.main:app --reload --port 8000
   145→
   146→# Frontend
   147→cd frontend && npm install && npm run dev
   148→
   149→# Type-check (required before merge)
   150→cd frontend && npx tsc --noEmit
   151→
   152→# Validate oscar.json
   153→python backend/validator.py
   154→```
   155→

1→## Oscar Medical Guidelines → PDF Scraper + “Initial Criteria” Tree Explorer (1 hour + 30 min Q/A)
     2→
     3→### Goal
     4→Build a small end-to-end system that:
     5→
     6→- Discovers and downloads **all Medical guideline PDFs** linked from Oscar’s medical clinical guidelines page.
     7→- Uses an LLM to structure **at least 10** guidelines’ **initial** medical necessity criteria into JSON decision trees like `oscar.json`.
     8→- Persists both the scraped policy metadata and the structured tree in a database.
     9→- Provides a UI to browse policies and clearly navigate/render the criteria tree.
    10→
    11→Source page: [Oscar Clinical Guidelines: Medical](https://www.hioscar.com/clinical-guidelines/medical)
    12→
    13→Example “multiple trees / initial vs continuation” policy page: [`https://www.hioscar.com/medical/cg013v11`](https://www.hioscar.com/medical/cg013v11)
    14→
    15→Timebox: **120 minutes implementation + 30 minutes Q/A**.
    16→
    17→---
    18→
    19→### What you are building (high level)
    20→Your solution must include the following components (implementation details are up to you):
    21→
    22→- **PDF discovery**: identify every medical guideline PDF link from the source page.
    23→- **PDF download**: download each discovered PDF and record success/failure.
    24→- **Structuring pipeline (at least 10 guidelines)**: pick at least 10 policy PDFs, extract text, use an LLM to produce structured criteria trees, validate them, and store them.
    25→- **UI**: list policies and render the structured criteria tree clearly.
    26→
    27→---
    28→
    29→### Data model requirements (minimum)
    30→You must store at least:
    31→
    32→- **Policies / guidelines (ALL PDFs discovered)**
    33→  - `title` (best-effort from link text / page)
    34→  - `pdf_url`
    35→  - `source_page_url` (the page where the PDF was found)
    36→  - `discovered_at`
    37→  - Uniqueness: `pdf_url` must be unique (reruns must be idempotent)
    38→
    39→- **Downloads (ALL PDFs)**
    40→  - `policy_id` (or equivalent link to the policy record)
    41→  - `stored_location` (file path or blob reference)
    42→  - `downloaded_at`
    43→  - `http_status` (or equivalent)
    44→  - `error` (nullable; store failure reason)
    45→
    46→- **Structured policies (AT LEAST 10)**
    47→  - `policy_id` (one of the policies you chose)
    48→  - `extracted_text` (or a reference to stored extracted text)
    49→  - `structured_json` (the criteria tree)
    50→  - `structured_at`
    51→  - `llm_metadata` (model name and/or prompt; minimal is fine)
    52→  - `validation_error` (nullable; store schema validation failures)
    53→
    54→---
    55→
    56→### Structured JSON format (required)
    57→Your structured output must match the shape of `oscar.json` in this repo.
    58→
    59→At minimum:
    60→
    61→- Top level:
    62→  - `title` (string)
    63→  - `insurance_name` (string; set to `Oscar Health`)
    64→  - `rules` (object; root node)
    65→
    66→- `rules` node shape (recursive):
    67→  - `rule_id` (string)
    68→  - `rule_text` (string)
    69→  - optional `operator` (string; `AND` or `OR`)
    70→  - optional `rules` (array of child nodes)
    71→
    72→Notes:
    73→
    74→- Leaf nodes have `rule_id` + `rule_text`.
    75→- Non-leaf nodes should include an `operator` and a `rules` array.
    76→
    77→---
    78→
    79→### Critical constraint: “initial only”
    80→Some policies include:
    81→
    82→- Separate **Initial** and **Continuation** criteria, and/or
    83→- Multiple distinct criteria trees (e.g., multiple indications or pathways)
    84→
    85→You must structure and store **at least 10 trees**, each representing the **initial** criteria of a different guideline.
    86→
    87→You must:
    88→
    89→- Implement a reasonable selection method (heuristics are allowed).
    90→- Document your approach in your README section “Initial-only selection logic”.
    91→
    92→If you can’t reliably detect “initial”, you may fallback to a deterministic heuristic (example: “first complete criteria tree”), but you must clearly explain it.
    93→
    94→---
    95→
    96→### Functional requirements (acceptance criteria)
    97→
    98→#### A) PDF discovery (ALL)
    99→- From the source page, discover **every** PDF link for medical guidelines.
   100→- Store each in the DB with required metadata.
   101→- Reruns must not duplicate existing records.
   102→
   103→#### B) PDF download (ALL)
   104→- Download every discovered PDF.
   105→- Persist download outcomes (success/failure) and where the PDF is stored.
   106→- Must include basic retry + rate limiting (lightweight is fine).
   107→
   108→#### C) Structuring pipeline (AT LEAST 10 guidelines)
   109→- Choose at least 10 discovered policies and structure them.
   110→- Extract text from the PDF and feed it to an LLM.
   111→- Validate the LLM output against the required schema.
   112→- Store:
   113→  - extracted text (or reference)
   114→  - validated structured JSON
   115→  - LLM metadata (minimum: model identifier)
   116→
   117→#### D) UI (policy navigation + tree rendering)
   118→- Show a list of discovered policies (at least title + PDF link).
   119→- Indicate whether a policy has a structured tree.
   120→- Provide a detail view for the structured policy that:
   121→  - shows policy title + links (source and/or PDF)
   122→  - renders the criteria as a navigable tree
   123→  - supports expand/collapse per node (minimum)
   124→  - clearly distinguishes operator nodes (`AND` / `OR`) from leaf criteria
   125→
   126→---
   127→
   128→### Non-functional requirements
   129→- **Polite scraping**: include throttling and retries; avoid hammering the site.
   130→- **Deterministic reruns**: discovery and download steps should be safe to re-run.
   131→- **Error visibility**: failures should be visible in logs and persisted where relevant.
   132→
   133→---
   134→
   135→### Deliverables
   136→At the end of 60 minutes, the reviewer should be able to:
   137→
   138→- Confirm the DB contains **all** discovered PDF records from the source page.
   139→- Confirm PDFs were downloaded (or see recorded failure reasons).
   140→- View **at least 10** structured JSON trees stored in the DB, matching `oscar.json` shape.
   141→- Open the UI and browse policies, and view the structured tree clearly.
   142→
   143→Your repo must include:
   144→
   145→- This README updated with:
   146→  - Setup instructions (prereqs)
   147→  - How to run: discovery, download, structuring, UI
   148→  - Which policy you structured
   149→  - “Initial-only selection logic” explanation
   150→- An example environment file (`.env.example`) containing placeholders for any secrets (no real keys committed). The required LLM API key is referenced in `.env.example`.
   151→
   152→---
   153→
   154→### What we’ll cover in the 30-minute Q/A
   155→- How you ensured PDF discovery completeness on the source page.
   156→- How you handled retries, throttling, and idempotency.
   157→- Your “initial-only” selection logic and its failure modes.
   158→- How you validated LLM output and handled malformed JSON.
   159→- Your UI approach to rendering large nested criteria trees.
   160→
   161→---
   162→
   163→## Implementation
   164→
   165→### Prerequisites
   166→
   167→- Python 3.12+
   168→- Node.js 18+
   169→- An OpenAI API key (GPT-4o)
   170→
   171→### Setup
   172→
   173→```bash
   174→# Clone and enter project
   175→git clone https://github.com/gianyrox/full-stack-feb.git
   176→cd full-stack-feb
   177→
   178→# Backend
   179→cd backend
   180→pip install -r requirements.txt
   181→cp ../.env.example ../.env   # Edit .env with your OPENAI_API_KEY
   182→cd ..
   183→
   184→# Frontend
   185→cd frontend
   186→npm install
   187→cd ..
   188→```
   189→
   190→### How to run
   191→
   192→```bash
   193→# 1. Start the backend (auto-creates DB and runs migrations)
   194→cd backend && uvicorn backend.main:app --reload --port 8000
   195→
   196→# 2. Start the frontend (in a separate terminal)
   197→cd frontend && npm run dev
   198→
   199→# 3. Run the full pipeline via the UI or API:
   200→#    POST /api/run-scraper     — discovers + downloads all PDFs
   201→#    POST /api/run-structurer  — extracts text + structures at least 10 with LLM
   202→
   203→# Or run from the command line:
   204→python -m backend.scraper          # Discovery + download
   205→python -m backend.structurer 10    # Structure 10 guidelines
   206→```
   207→
   208→The UI is available at `http://localhost:5173`. The API is at `http://localhost:8000`.
   209→
   210→### Architecture
   211→
   212→| Layer | Tech | Key Files |
   213→|-------|------|-----------|
   214→| Backend | FastAPI + SQLAlchemy Core (raw SQL) | `backend/main.py`, `backend/scraper.py`, `backend/structurer.py` |
   215→| Database | SQLite (WAL mode) | `backend/sql/sqlite_schema.sql`, `data/app.db` |
   216→| Text Extraction | PyMuPDF (fitz) | `backend/extractor.py` |
   217→| LLM | OpenAI GPT-4o (JSON Mode) | `backend/structurer.py` |
   218→| Validation | Pydantic + DFS traversal | `backend/validator.py` |
   219→| Frontend | Vite + React + TypeScript + Tailwind | `frontend/src/` |
   220→
   221→### Which policies were structured
   222→
   223→The pipeline selects the first 10+ policies that have successful downloads but no existing structured entry. Selection is deterministic (ordered by policy ID). On each run, it picks up where it left off.
   224→
   225→### Initial-only selection logic
   226→
   227→Two-layer defense to extract only the **initial** medical necessity criteria:
   228→
   229→**Layer 1: Regex state machine (pre-LLM)**
   230→
   231→`extract_initial_section()` in `backend/extractor.py` scans the extracted text line-by-line:
   232→
   233→- **START patterns**: `initial (criteria|authorization|approval)`, `criteria for medically necessary`, `medical necessity criteria`, `conditions for coverage`, `clinical indications`
   234→- **END patterns**: `continuation`, `re-authorization`, `renewal`, `repair/revision`, `experimental/investigational`, `applicable billing codes`, `HCPCS & CPT codes`, `Medical Necessity Criteria for Reauthorization`
   235→
   236→Returns a `SectionResult` with:
   237→- **confidence 0.95**: explicit “Initial” keyword found in header
   238→- **confidence 0.85**: generic criteria header found (e.g., CG008 Bariatric which doesn’t say “Initial”)
   239→- **confidence 0.30**: no boundary detected — full text passed to LLM as fallback
   240→
   241→**Layer 2: LLM prompt instructions**
   242→
   243→The system prompt explicitly instructs GPT-4o to:
   244→- Parse ONLY initial criteria
   245→- IGNORE sections labeled Continuation, Reauthorization, Repair/Revision, Experimental, Billing Codes
   246→- Stop extracting at those boundaries
   247→- Default to the first criteria tree if no explicit “Initial” label exists
   248→
   249→**Failure modes:**
   250→- Documents with non-standard section headers → confidence drops to 0.30, full text sent to LLM
   251→- LLM extracts wrong section → semantic retry feeds the validation error back, up to 3 attempts
   252→- Ambiguous multi-indication trees → extracts only the first/primary one per the prompt
   253→
   254→### Validation approach
   255→
   256→Pydantic recursive models enforce schema structure. Beyond that, a DFS tree traversal (`_traverse_and_validate` in `backend/validator.py`) checks:
   257→- Unique rule IDs across the tree
   258→- Hierarchy prefix consistency (child IDs start with parent prefix)
   259→- Sequential child numbering (1.1, 1.2, 1.3 — no gaps)
   260→- Empty rule_text detection
   261→- Depth > 5 warning (possible hallucination)
   262→- Operator/text consistency (“all of the following” → AND)
   263→
   264→Malformed LLM JSON is auto-repaired via `fast-json-repair` before validation. Hallucinated operators on leaf nodes are stripped by `clean_leaf_nodes()`.
   265→

1→# BEADS — Autonomous Work Units
     2→
     3→Each bead is a self-contained, testable unit. Beads can run in parallel where dependencies allow.
     4→
     5→## Dependency Graph
     6→
     7→```
     8→[B1: Scaffold] ──┬──→ [B2: Discovery] ──→ [B3: PDF Resolver + Downloader] ──→ [B5: Text Extractor]
     9→                 │                                                                     │
    10→                 │                                                            [B5.5: Section Extractor]
    11→                 │                                                                     │
    12→                 │                                                             [B6: LLM Pipeline]
    13→                 │                                                                     │
    14→                 │                                                             [B7: Validator]
    15→                 │
    16→                 ├──→ [B4: Database Layer] (parallel with B2)
    17→                 │
    18→                 └──→ [B8: API Server] ──→ [B9: UI List] ──→ [B10: UI Tree]
    19→```
    20→
    21→## Bead Status
    22→
    23→### B1: Project Scaffold — DONE
    24→- FastAPI + Vite/React/TS + SQLite
    25→- All deps installed, dev servers boot
    26→
    27→### B4: Database Layer — DONE (upgraded with research)
    28→- SQLAlchemy Core + raw SQL (not ORM) per research recommendation
    29→- SQLite with WAL mode, foreign keys, busy_timeout=5000
    30→- Schema in `backend/sql/sqlite_schema.sql` with CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS
    31→- UPSERT patterns: `ON CONFLICT(pdf_url) DO UPDATE` for policies, `ON CONFLICT(policy_id) DO UPDATE` for structured_policies
    32→- Auto-migration on FastAPI startup via `backend/migrate.py`
    33→
    34→### B7: JSON Schema Validator — DONE (Gemini DFS enhancements applied)
    35→- Pydantic recursive models (RuleNode, StructuredPolicy)
    36→- Validates operator+rules consistency (both present or both absent)
    37→- `validate_structured_json(data) → (bool, Optional[str])`
    38→- **Gemini DFS enhancements — IMPLEMENTED in `backend/validator.py`**:
    39→  - `_traverse_and_validate()`: DFS traversal with unique rule_id check, hierarchy prefix validation
    40→  - Sequential child ID check (1.1, 1.2, 1.3 not 1.1, 1.3)
    41→  - Empty rule_text detection (raises ValueError)
    42→  - Depth > 5 warning (possible hallucination)
    43→  - Operator consistency check ("all of the following" → AND, "one of the following" → OR)
    44→  - Returns warnings separately from errors (True + warnings vs False + error)
    45→
    46→### B8: API Server — DONE (upgraded with research)
    47→- 4 endpoints with raw SQL queries:
    48→  - `GET /api/policies` — list with LEFT JOIN latest_download + structured existence
    49→  - `GET /api/policies/:id` — detail with download + structured data
    50→  - `GET /api/policies/:id/tree` — structured JSON tree directly
    51→  - `GET /api/stats` — counts for policies/downloaded/failed/structured
    52→- CORS for Vite dev server (localhost:5173)
    53→- `_parse_json_maybe()` handles SQLite TEXT ↔ Python dict
    54→
    55→### B9: UI — Policy List — DONE (upgraded with research)
    56→- Table with title (linked), PDF link, download badge, structured badge
    57→- Search filter by title
    58→- react-router-dom Link to detail view
    59→
    60→### B10: UI — Tree Viewer — DONE (upgraded with research)
    61→- Custom recursive component (no library needed for ~50-100 nodes)
    62→- Expand/collapse per node, default expand depth 0-1
    63→- AND = blue badge, OR = orange badge, leaf = checkmark icon
    64→- Expand All / Collapse All buttons
    65→- Legend bar, rule_id monospace labels, connector lines
    66→- React.memo for performance
    67→
    68→### B2: PDF Discovery — DONE (implemented in `backend/scraper.py`)
    69→- **Depends**: B1, B4
    70→- **Source**: `https://www.hioscar.com/clinical-guidelines/medical`
    71→- **Architecture**: Single-page listing, NO pagination. All guidelines visible in one HTML response.
    72→- **Sections on page**: "Upcoming Policy Changes", "Medical Guidelines" (main list), "Adopted Guidelines"
    73→- **Discovery strategy**:
    74→  1. Parse listing page HTML with requests + BeautifulSoup
    75→  2. Find all `<a>` tags where visible text is exactly `"PDF"` (not "LINK")
    76→  3. Each PDF link's `href` points to an **intermediate policy page** (NOT a direct PDF)
    77→  4. Derive title from nearest `<li>` parent text, strip trailing "PDF"
    78→  5. Title format: `"Acupuncture (CG013, Ver. 11)"` — parse code/version with tolerant regex (optional)
    79→- **CRITICAL**: Links do NOT go directly to `.pdf` files. They go to intermediate pages like:
    80→  - `/medical/cg013v11`, `/medical/cg057v8` (Medical Guidelines)
    81→  - `/pharmacy/pg193v2`, `/pharmacy/cg059v7` (some CG items under /pharmacy/)
    82→  - `/medical/adopted/asam` (Adopted Guidelines)
    83→  - Do NOT assume CG → /medical/ and PG → /pharmacy/. Follow href as ground truth.
    84→- **Dedup**: Listing page has duplicates (nested + top-level). Dedupe by intermediate URL.
    85→- **Filter**: Only process entries with "PDF" link text, skip "LINK" entries (external sites).
    86→- **Store**: UPSERT into policies table with `intermediate_url` as the discovered link
    87→- **Polite**: 0.5s delay + jitter between requests, browser-like User-Agent, 3 retries with backoff
    88→- **robots.txt**: `Allow: /`, no Crawl-delay, clinical guideline pages not disallowed
    89→
    90→### B3: PDF Resolver + Downloader — DONE (implemented in `backend/scraper.py`)
    91→- **Depends**: B2, B4
    92→- **Two-step process** (this is the key insight from ChatGPT research):
    93→  1. **Resolve**: Fetch each intermediate page, parse `__NEXT_DATA__` script tag for real PDF URL
    94→  2. **Download**: Stream-download the actual PDF from `assets.ctfassets.net`
    95→- **PDF URL Resolution** (from intermediate page):
    96→  1. Parse HTML, find `<script id="__NEXT_DATA__">`
    97→  2. `json.loads()` the script content
    98→  3. Recursively walk the JSON for strings containing `ctfassets.net`
    99→  4. Prefer URLs ending in `.pdf`; accept extensionless as fallback
   100→  5. Regex fallback: scan raw HTML for `https?://assets\.ctfassets\.net/[^\s"']+`
   101→- **Real PDF URLs** are on Contentful CDN: `https://assets.ctfassets.net/<space_id>/<asset_id>/<hash>/<filename>.pdf`
   102→- **Download**: Stream with `iter_content(chunk_size=64KB)`, verify `Content-Type: application/pdf`
   103→- **Sanity check**: Reject files < 200 bytes (likely error pages)
   104→- **Size variability**: 6 pages (small) to 113 pages (large) — must handle both
   105→- **Store**: `data/pdfs/` dir, record in downloads table (http_status, error, stored_location)
   106→- **Rate limiting**: 0.5s delay between requests, 3 retries with exponential backoff
   107→- **Session**: reuse `requests.Session()` with browser-like headers for connection pooling
   108→
   109→### B5: PDF Text Extractor — DONE (implemented in `backend/extractor.py`)
   110→- **Depends**: B3
   111→- **DECISION: Use PyMuPDF (fitz)** — Gemini's comparative analysis is definitive:
   112→  - Flawless Unicode (≥, ≤, ™) — critical for medical thresholds like "BMI ≥ 40"
   113→  - Fastest: < 100ms for 10 pages vs pdfplumber ~1-2s
   114→  - `sort=True` enforces natural reading order, preserving list hierarchy
   115→  - C-based MuPDF engine, superior memory efficiency
   116→- Implementation: `page.get_text("text", sort=True)` per page, join with newlines
   117→- Handle multi-page, preserve numbered list hierarchy (1, a, i)
   118→- Strip headers/footers
   119→- **Grok suggested pdfplumber** but Gemini's analysis shows PyMuPDF better for hierarchical text (pdfplumber better for tables/financial docs)
   120→
   121→### B5.5: Initial-Only Section Extractor — DONE (in `backend/extractor.py`)
   122→- **Depends**: B5
   123→- **Purpose**: Pre-slice PDF text to only the initial criteria BEFORE sending to LLM
   124→- **Implementation**: `extract_initial_criteria(full_text) → (text, confidence, logic_log)`
   125→- **State machine approach** (line-by-line scanning, NOT multi-line regex):
   126→  - START_PATTERNS: `criteria for medically necessary`, `initial (criteria|authorization|approval)`, `medical necessity criteria`, `conditions for coverage`
   127→  - END_PATTERNS: `continuation`, `re-authorization`, `renewal`, `repair/revision`, `experimental/investigational`, `applicable billing codes`, `HCPCS & CPT`
   128→  - Fallback: uppercase headers like "BACKGROUND" or "SUMMARY" as section breaks
   129→- **Confidence scoring**:
   130→  - 0.95 = explicit "Initial" keyword found
   131→  - 0.85 = generic "Criteria for Medically Necessary" found (e.g., CG008 Bariatric)
   132→  - 0.30 = no boundary found, full text passed to LLM (flags for human review)
   133→- **Store confidence + extraction_logic as metadata** for pipeline observability
   134→- Full implementation in `research/2gemini.md` Part B
   135→
   136→### B6: LLM Structuring Pipeline — DONE (implemented in `backend/structurer.py`)
   137→- **Depends**: B5.5, B7
   138→- **API**: OpenAI GPT-4o with `response_format={"type": "json_object"}` (JSON Mode)
   139→  - **NOT Structured Outputs** — Gemini confirmed OpenAI rejects recursive Pydantic models
   140→  - `temperature=0.1` for deterministic analytical mapping
   141→  - `max_tokens=4096`
   142→- **System prompt**: Full medical policy analyst prompt from Gemini (see `research/2gemini.md` Part A)
   143→  - Covers: section identification, hierarchical rule_id generation (dotted notation), leaf vs non-leaf rules, AND/OR detection (explicit triggers, inline connectors, implicit convention), edge cases (cross-refs, inline exceptions, notes)
   144→- **User prompt**: Injects extracted text in `<EXTRACTED_TEXT>` tags with 6-point instruction checklist
   145→- **Initial-only heuristic** (combined from all research):
   146→  - Pre-extraction via B5.5 (regex state machine with confidence scoring)
   147→  - LLM prompt also instructs to stop at continuation/repair/billing boundaries
   148→  - Double protection: regex + prompt
   149→- **Semantic retry logic** (from Gemini):
   150→  - Up to 3 attempts per PDF
   151→  - On validation failure, feed error message back to LLM in next attempt
   152→  - If wrong section detected, append "CRITICAL ERROR: You extracted Continuation criteria"
   153→- **JSON repair** (from Gemini): Use `fast-json-repair` library for malformed LLM output
   154→  - Strip markdown fencing, repair missing brackets/commas
   155→  - Validate repaired JSON with B7
   156→- **Post-processing**: `clean_leaf_nodes()` — remove hallucinated operators from leaf nodes
   157→- **Batch processing**: `asyncio.gather` for concurrent PDF processing
   158→- **Cost**: ~$0.018/PDF, ~$0.28 for 15 PDFs
   159→- Process at least 10 guidelines
   160→- Validate with B7 (Pydantic) before storing
   161→- Store: extracted_text ref, structured_json, llm_metadata (model + prompt version), validation_error, confidence_score
   162→
   163→## Research Findings Applied
   164→
   165→### Prompt 1 — ChatGPT Deep Research (Scraping Architecture) — NEW
   166→**CRITICAL FINDINGS** that changed B2 and B3:
   167→1. PDF links on listing page → intermediate pages (NOT direct PDFs)
   168→2. Intermediate pages are Next.js SSR — visible HTML is empty, data in `__NEXT_DATA__`
   169→3. Real PDFs on `assets.ctfassets.net` (Contentful CDN)
   170→4. Must parse `__NEXT_DATA__` JSON → recursive walk for `ctfassets.net` URLs
   171→5. Some CG items live under `/pharmacy/` not `/medical/` — follow href, don't assume
   172→6. Listing page has duplicates — dedupe by URL
   173→7. `robots.txt` allows crawling, no Crawl-delay specified
   174→8. PDF sizes vary from 6 to 113 pages
   175→9. PDF headings: "Medical Necessity Criteria for Initial Authorization" vs "Reauthorization"
   176→10. Complete scraper skeleton provided in `research/1chatgpt.md`
   177→
   178→### Prompt 3 — ChatGPT (Stack Architecture)
   179→- Confirmed: FastAPI + SQLAlchemy Core (raw SQL) + SQLite WAL
   180→- Confirmed: Vite + React + TS + React Router + Tailwind
   181→- Confirmed: Custom recursive TreeViewer

   187→
   188→### Gemini Research (`research/2gemini.md`) — NEW
   189→**Production-grade pipeline blueprint with code. Key contributions:**
   190→1. **System prompt**: Full medical policy analyst prompt with exhaustive edge case handling (Part A)
   191→2. **PDF extraction**: PyMuPDF (fitz) > pdfplumber for clinical guidelines — Unicode, speed, sort=True (Part C)
   192→3. **Section extraction**: State machine with regex START/END patterns + confidence scoring (Part B)
   193→4. **JSON repair**: `fast-json-repair` library for malformed LLM output (Part D)
   194→5. **Tree validation**: DFS traversal with hierarchy prefix, uniqueness, depth, sequential checks (Part D)
   195→6. **OpenAI integration**: JSON Mode (NOT Structured Outputs — recursive schemas rejected), temperature=0.1 (Part E)
   196→7. **Semantic retry**: Feed validation errors back to LLM for self-correction, up to 3 attempts (Part E)
   197→8. **Async batch processing**: `asyncio.gather` for concurrent PDF processing (Part E)
   198→9. **Cost estimate**: ~$0.018/PDF, ~$0.28 for batch of 15 (Part E)
   199→10. **New bead B5.5**: Initial-only section extractor (pre-LLM regex slicing)

   253→        )
   254→
   255→    return PolicyDetail(
   256→        id=policy_id,
   257→        title=row["title"],
   258→        pdf_url=row["pdf_url"],
   259→        source_page_url=row["source_page_url"],
   260→        discovered_at=str(row["discovered_at"]),
   261→        download_status=download_status,
   262→        latest_download=latest_download,
   263→        structured=structured,
   264→    )
   265→
   266→@app.get("/api/policies/{policy_id}/tree")
   267→def get_policy_tree(policy_id: int, conn: Connection = Depends(get_conn)) -> Any:
   268→    row = conn.execute(text(TREE_SQL), {"policy_id": policy_id}).mappings().first()
   269→    if not row:
   270→        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Structured tree not found for this policy")
   271→    try:
   272→        return _parse_json_maybe(row["structured_json"])
   273→    except json.JSONDecodeError:
   274→        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="Stored structured_json is not valid JSON")
   275→
   276→@app.get("/api/stats", response_model=StatsResponse)
   277→def get_stats(conn: Connection = Depends(get_conn)) -> StatsResponse:
   278→    row = conn.execute(text(STATS_SQL)).mappings().first()
   279→    if not row:
   280→        return StatsResponse(total_policies=0, total_downloaded=0, total_failed=0, total_structured=0)
   281→    return StatsResponse(
   282→        total_policies=int(row["total_policies"] or 0),
   283→        total_downloaded=int(row["total_downloaded"] or 0),
   284→        total_failed=int(row["total_failed"] or 0),
   285→        total_structured=int(row["total_structured"] or 0),
   286→    )
   287→
   288→@app.post("/api/run-scraper")
   289→def run_scraper():
   290→    """Trigger the B2+B3 scraper pipeline (discover + resolve + download)."""
   291→    from backend.scraper import run_full_pipeline
   292→    return run_full_pipeline()
   293→
   294→

   296→def run_structurer():

1→"""
     2→B2: PDF Discovery + B3: PDF Resolver & Downloader
     3→
     4→Two-phase pipeline:
     5→  1. Discover intermediate policy page URLs from the Oscar listing page
     6→  2. Resolve each to a real PDF URL (via __NEXT_DATA__) and download
     7→
     8→Based on ChatGPT deep research findings (research/1chatgpt.md).
     9→"""
    10→
    11→from __future__ import annotations
    12→
    13→import json
    14→import os
    15→import re
    16→import time
    17→import random
    18→import logging
    19→from dataclasses import dataclass
    20→from pathlib import Path
    21→from typing import Optional
    22→from urllib.parse import urljoin
    23→
    24→import requests
    25→from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
    26→from sqlalchemy import create_engine, event, text
    27→from sqlalchemy.engine import Engine
    28→
    29→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    30→
    31→# ── Config ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    32→
    33→SOURCE_URL = "https://www.hioscar.com/clinical-guidelines/medical"
    34→BASE_URL = "https://www.hioscar.com"
    35→PDF_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "data" / "pdfs"
    36→
    37→UA = (
    38→    "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) "
    39→    "AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) "
    40→    "Chrome/[IP-REDACTED] Safari/537.36"
    41→)
    42→
    43→PDF_URL_RE = re.compile(r'((?:https?:)?//assets\.ctfassets\.net/[^\s"\']+)', re.I)
    44→
    45→# ── HTTP Session ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    46→
    47→SESSION = requests.Session()
    48→SESSION.headers.update({
    49→    "User-Agent": UA,
    50→    "Accept": "text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8",
    51→    "Accept-Language": "en-US,en;q=0.9",
    52→    "Connection": "keep-alive",
    53→})
    54→
    55→# ── Data Types ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    56→
    57→
    58→@dataclass(frozen=True)
    59→class PolicyLink:
    60→    title: str
    61→    intermediate_url: str
    62→
    63→
    64→@dataclass
    65→class ResolvedPolicy:
    66→    title: str
    67→    intermediate_url: str
    68→    pdf_url: Optional[str]
    69→    error: Optional[str] = None
    70→
    71→
    72→# ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    73→
    74→
    75→def _sleep_polite(base: float = 0.5) -> None:
    76→    time.sleep(base + random.uniform(0.0, 0.35))
    77→
    78→
    79→def _fetch_with_retries(url: str, *, max_retries: int = 3, base_delay: float = 0.5) -> requests.Response:
    80→    last_err: Optional[Exception] = None
    81→    for attempt in range(1, max_retries + 1):
    82→        try:
    83→            if attempt > 1:
    84→                time.sleep(base_delay * attempt)
    85→            resp = SESSION.get(url, timeout=30)
    86→            resp.raise_for_status()
    87→            return resp
    88→        except Exception as e:
    89→            last_err = e
    90→            logger.warning("Attempt %d/%d failed for %s: %s", attempt, max_retries, url, e)
    91→    raise RuntimeError(f"Failed to fetch {url} after {max_retries} retries") from last_err
    92→
    93→
    94→def _safe_filename(s: str, max_len: int = 140) -> str:
    95→    s = re.sub(r"[^\w\s\-.()&]", "", s)
    96→    s = re.sub(r"\s+", "_", s).strip("_")
    97→    return (s[:max_len] or "policy")
    98→
    99→
   100→def _normalize_url(url: str) -> str:
   101→    """Fix protocol-relative URLs (//assets...) to https."""
   102→    if url.startswith("//"):
   103→        return "https:" + url
   104→    return url
   105→
   106→
   107→# ── B2: Discovery ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   108→
   109→
   110→def discover_intermediate_links() -> list[PolicyLink]:
   111→    """Parse the Oscar listing page and find all PDF intermediate links.
   112→
   113→    Strategy:
   114→      - Find <a> elements whose visible text is exactly 'PDF'
   115→      - Use each href as the intermediate URL
   116→      - Derive title from nearest <li> parent text
   117→      - Dedupe by intermediate URL
   118→    """
   119→    logger.info("Discovering policies from %s", SOURCE_URL)
   120→    resp = _fetch_with_retries(SOURCE_URL, max_retries=3, base_delay=0.0)
   121→    soup = BeautifulSoup(resp.text, "html.parser")
   122→
   123→    results: list[PolicyLink] = []
   124→    seen: set[str] = set()
   125→
   126→    for a in soup.find_all("a", href=True):
   127→        if a.get_text(strip=True) != "PDF":
   128→            continue
   129→
   130→        href = a["href"].strip()
   131→        intermediate_url = urljoin(BASE_URL, href)
   132→
   133→        if intermediate_url in seen:
   134→            continue
   135→        seen.add(intermediate_url)
   136→
   137→        # Title: parent <li> text minus trailing "PDF"
   138→        li = a.find_parent("li")
   139→        if li:
   140→            title = li.get_text(" ", strip=True)
   141→            title = re.sub(r"\s*PDF\s*$", "", title).strip()
   142→        else:
   143→            prev = a.find_previous(string=True)
   144→            title = prev.strip() if prev else intermediate_url
   145→
   146→        results.append(PolicyLink(title=title, intermediate_url=intermediate_url))
   147→
   148→    logger.info("Discovered %d unique intermediate links", len(results))
   149→    return results
   150→
   151→
   152→# ── B3: PDF URL Resolution ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   153→
   154→
   155→def extract_pdf_url(intermediate_html: str) -> Optional[str]:
   156→    """Extract the real PDF URL from an intermediate policy page.
   157→
   158→    Preferred: parse __NEXT_DATA__ JSON and search for ctfassets.net URLs.
   159→    Fallback: regex scan raw HTML.
   160→    """
   161→    soup = BeautifulSoup(intermediate_html, "html.parser")
   162→    script = soup.find("script", id="__NEXT_DATA__")
   163→
   164→    # 1) Next.js payload
   165→    if script and script.string:
   166→        try:
   167→            data = json.loads(script.string)
   168→            stack = [data]
   169→            hits: list[str] = []
   170→            while stack:
   171→                obj = stack.pop()
   172→                if isinstance(obj, dict):
   173→                    stack.extend(obj.values())
   174→                elif isinstance(obj, list):
   175→                    stack.extend(obj)
   176→                elif isinstance(obj, str) and "ctfassets.net" in obj:
   177→                    hits.append(obj)
   178→
   179→            pdf_hits = [h for h in hits if h.lower().endswith(".pdf")]
   180→            if pdf_hits:
   181→                return _normalize_url(pdf_hits[0])
   182→            if hits:
   183→                return _normalize_url(hits[0])
   184→        except json.JSONDecodeError:
   185→            pass
   186→
   187→    # 2) Regex fallback
   188→    matches = PDF_URL_RE.findall(intermediate_html)
   189→    if not matches:
   190→        return None
   191→
   192→    pdf_matches = [m for m in matches if m.lower().endswith(".pdf")]
   193→    result = pdf_matches[0] if pdf_matches else matches[0]
   194→    return _normalize_url(result)
   195→
   196→
   197→def resolve_policies(links: list[PolicyLink]) -> list[ResolvedPolicy]:
   198→    """For each intermediate link, fetch the page and extract the real PDF URL."""
   199→    resolved: list[ResolvedPolicy] = []
   200→
   201→    for idx, link in enumerate(links, 1):
   202→        logger.info("[%d/%d] Resolving: %s", idx, len(links), link.title)
   203→        _sleep_polite(0.5)
   204→
   205→        try:
   206→            resp = _fetch_with_retries(link.intermediate_url, max_retries=3, base_delay=0.5)
   207→            pdf_url = extract_pdf_url(resp.text)
   208→
   209→            if not pdf_url:
   210→                logger.warning("  No PDF URL found on %s", link.intermediate_url)
   211→                resolved.append(ResolvedPolicy(
   212→                    title=link.title,
   213→                    intermediate_url=link.intermediate_url,
   214→                    pdf_url=None,
   215→                    error="No PDF URL found in __NEXT_DATA__ or HTML",
   216→                ))
   217→            else:
   218→                logger.info("  -> PDF: %s", pdf_url[:80])
   219→                resolved.append(ResolvedPolicy(
   220→                    title=link.title,
   221→                    intermediate_url=link.intermediate_url,
   222→                    pdf_url=pdf_url,
   223→                ))
   224→        except Exception as e:
   225→            logger.error("  !! Failed to resolve %s: %s", link.intermediate_url, e)
   226→            resolved.append(ResolvedPolicy(
   227→                title=link.title,
   228→                intermediate_url=link.intermediate_url,
   229→                pdf_url=None,
   230→                error=str(e),
   231→            ))
   232→
   233→    return resolved
   234→
   235→
   236→# ── B3: PDF Download ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   237→
   238→
   239→def download_pdf(pdf_url: str, out_dir: Path, filename_stem: str) -> str:
   240→    """Stream-download a PDF. Returns the saved file path."""
   241→    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   242→    out_path = out_dir / f"{_safe_filename(filename_stem)}.pdf"
   243→
   244→    with SESSION.get(pdf_url, timeout=60, stream=True) as r:
   245→        r.raise_for_status()
   246→        ctype = r.headers.get("Content-Type", "")
   247→        if "pdf" not in ctype.lower() and "octet-stream" not in ctype.lower():
   248→            raise RuntimeError(f"Unexpected Content-Type {ctype} for {pdf_url}")
   249→
   250→        with open(out_path, "wb") as f:
   251→            for chunk in r.iter_content(chunk_size=64 * 1024):
   252→                if chunk:
   253→                    f.write(chunk)
   254→
   255→    if out_path.stat().st_size < 200:
   256→        raise RuntimeError(f"Downloaded file too small ({out_path.stat().st_size}B), likely error page")
   257→
   258→    return str(out_path)
   259→
   260→
   261→# ── Database Integration ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
   262→
   263→
   264→def _make_engine() -> Engine:
   265→    db_url = os.getenv("DATABASE_URL", "sqlite+pysqlite:///./data/app.db")
   266→    engine = create_engine(
   267→        db_url,
   268→        connect_args={"check_same_thread": False} if db_url.startswith("sqlite") else {},
   269→        pool_pre_ping=True,
   270→    )
   271→    if db_url.startswith("sqlite"):
   272→        @event.listens_for(engine, "connect")
   273→        def _set_pragmas(dbapi_conn, _):
   274→            cur = dbapi_conn.cursor()
   275→            cur.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON;")
   276→            cur.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL;")
   277→            cur.execute("PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000;")
   278→            cur.close()
   279→    return engine
   280→
   281→
   282→UPSERT_POLICY_SQL = """
   283→INSERT INTO policies (title, pdf_url, source_page_url)
   284→VALUES (:title, :pdf_url, :source_page_url)
   285→ON CONFLICT(pdf_url) DO UPDATE SET
   286→    title = excluded.title,
   287→    source_page_url = excluded.source_page_url
   288→"""
   289→
   290→INSERT_DOWNLOAD_SQL = """
   291→INSERT INTO downloads (policy_id, stored_location, http_status, error)
   292→VALUES (:policy_id, :stored_location, :http_status, :error)
   293→"""
   294→
   295→GET_POLICY_ID_SQL = """
   296→SELECT id FROM policies WHERE pdf_url = :pdf_url
   297→"""
   298→
   299→
   300→def run_full_pipeline() -> dict:
   301→    """Run the complete B2+B3 pipeline: discover → resolve → download → store.
   302→
   303→    Returns summary stats.
   304→    """
   305→    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(levelname)s %(message)s")
   306→
   307→    from backend.migrate import run_migrations
   308→    run_migrations()
   309→
   310→    engine = _make_engine()
   311→
   312→    # Phase 1: Discover intermediate links
   313→    links = discover_intermediate_links()
   314→
   315→    # Phase 2: Resolve to real PDF URLs
   316→    resolved = resolve_policies(links)
   317→
   318→    # Phase 3: UPSERT policies + download PDFs
   319→    stats = {"discovered": len(links), "resolved": 0, "downloaded": 0, "failed": 0}
   320→
   321→    with engine.begin() as conn:
   322→        for rp in resolved:
   323→            # Use real PDF URL if resolved, otherwise store intermediate_url
   324→            pdf_url = rp.pdf_url or rp.intermediate_url
   325→
   326→            # UPSERT policy
   327→            conn.execute(text(UPSERT_POLICY_SQL), {
   328→                "title": rp.title,
   329→                "pdf_url": pdf_url,
   330→                "source_page_url": SOURCE_URL,
   331→            })
   332→
   333→            # Get the policy ID
   334→            row = conn.execute(text(GET_POLICY_ID_SQL), {"pdf_url": pdf_url}).first()
   335→            policy_id = row[0]
   336→
   337→            if rp.pdf_url is None:
   338→                # No PDF URL resolved — record as failed download
   339→                conn.execute(text(INSERT_DOWNLOAD_SQL), {
   340→                    "policy_id": policy_id,
   341→                    "stored_location": "",
   342→                    "http_status": None,
   343→                    "error": rp.error or "Could not resolve PDF URL",
   344→                })
   345→                stats["failed"] += 1
   346→                continue
   347→
   348→            stats["resolved"] += 1
   349→
   350→            # Download the PDF
   351→            _sleep_polite(0.5)
   352→            try:
   353→                stored_path = download_pdf(rp.pdf_url, PDF_DIR, rp.title)
   354→                conn.execute(text(INSERT_DOWNLOAD_SQL), {
   355→                    "policy_id": policy_id,
   356→                    "stored_location": stored_path,
   357→                    "http_status": 200,
   358→                    "error": None,
   359→                })
   360→                stats["downloaded"] += 1
   361→                logger.info("  OK saved: %s", stored_path)
   362→            except Exception as e:
   363→                logger.error("  !! Download failed for %s: %s", rp.title, e)
   364→                conn.execute(text(INSERT_DOWNLOAD_SQL), {
   365→                    "policy_id": policy_id,
   366→                    "stored_location": "",
   367→                    "http_status": None,
   368→                    "error": str(e),
   369→                })
   370→                stats["failed"] += 1
   371→
   372→    logger.info("Pipeline complete: %s", stats)
   373→    return stats
   374→
   375→
   376→if __name__ == "__main__":
   377→    import sys
   378→    sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent))
   379→
   380→    from dotenv import load_dotenv
   381→    load_dotenv()
   382→
   383→    stats = run_full_pipeline()
   384→    print(f"\nDone! {stats}")
   385→

1→from __future__ import annotations
     2→
     3→import json
     4→from typing import List, Literal, Optional, Tuple
     5→
     6→from pydantic import BaseModel, model_validator
     7→
     8→
     9→class RuleNode(BaseModel):
    10→    rule_id: str
    11→    rule_text: str
    12→    operator: Optional[Literal["AND", "OR"]] = None
    13→    rules: Optional[List[RuleNode]] = None
    14→
    15→    @model_validator(mode="after")
    16→    def check_operator_rules_consistency(self) -> "RuleNode":
    17→        has_operator = self.operator is not None
    18→        has_rules = self.rules is not None
    19→        if has_operator and not has_rules:
    20→            raise ValueError(
    21→                f"Node '{self.rule_id}' has 'operator' but no 'rules' array"
    22→            )
    23→        if has_rules and not has_operator:
    24→            raise ValueError(
    25→                f"Node '{self.rule_id}' has 'rules' array but no 'operator'"
    26→            )
    27→        return self
    28→
    29→
    30→RuleNode.model_rebuild()
    31→
    32→
    33→class StructuredPolicy(BaseModel):
    34→    title: str
    35→    insurance_name: str
    36→    rules: RuleNode
    37→
    38→
    39→def validate_structured_json(data: dict) -> Tuple[bool, Optional[str]]:
    40→    """Validate a dict against the StructuredPolicy schema.
    41→
    42→    Returns (True, None) on success or (False, error_message) on failure.
    43→    Performs both Pydantic schema validation and logical tree traversal.
    44→    """
    45→    # 1. Pydantic schema validation
    46→    try:
    47→        StructuredPolicy.model_validate(data)
    48→    except Exception as e:
    49→        return False, str(e)
    50→
    51→    # 2. Logical tree traversal (DFS)
    52→    warnings: List[str] = []
    53→    try:
    54→        _traverse_and_validate(data.get("rules", {}), "", 1, set(), warnings)
    55→    except ValueError as e:
    56→        return False, str(e)
    57→
    58→    if warnings:
    59→        return True, "Warnings: " + "; ".join(warnings)
    60→
    61→    return True, None
    62→
    63→
    64→def _traverse_and_validate(
    65→    node: dict,
    66→    parent_prefix: str,
    67→    depth: int,
    68→    seen_ids: set,
    69→    warnings: List[str],
    70→) -> None:
    71→    """DFS traversal to validate tree logic beyond schema."""
    72→    rule_id = node.get("rule_id", "")
    73→    rule_text = node.get("rule_text", "")
    74→
    75→    # Empty text check
    76→    if not rule_text.strip():
    77→        raise ValueError(f"Empty rule_text at node {rule_id}")
    78→
    79→    # Depth warning
    80→    if depth > 5:
    81→        warnings.append(f"Depth {depth} at {rule_id} (possible hallucination)")
    82→
    83→    # Unique rule_id
    84→    if rule_id in seen_ids:
    85→        raise ValueError(f"Duplicate rule_id: {rule_id}")
    86→    seen_ids.add(rule_id)
    87→
    88→    # Hierarchy prefix check (skip root "1")
    89→    if rule_id != "1" and parent_prefix:
    90→        if not rule_id.startswith(parent_prefix + "."):
    91→            warnings.append(f"Hierarchy break: {rule_id} not child of {parent_prefix}")
    92→
    93→    # Operator consistency with text
    94→    has_rules = "rules" in node and isinstance(node.get("rules"), list) and len(node["rules"]) > 0
    95→    if has_rules:
    96→        op = node.get("operator", "")
    97→        text_lower = rule_text.lower()
    98→        if "all of the following" in text_lower and op != "AND":
    99→            warnings.append(f"{rule_id} implies ALL but operator is {op}")
   100→        if "one of the following" in text_lower and op != "OR":
   101→            warnings.append(f"{rule_id} implies ONE but operator is {op}")
   102→
   103→        # Sequential child ID check
   104→        for i, child in enumerate(node["rules"], start=1):
   105→            expected = f"{rule_id}.{i}"
   106→            actual = child.get("rule_id", "")
   107→            if actual != expected:
   108→                warnings.append(f"Sequential gap: got {actual}, expected {expected}")
   109→            _traverse_and_validate(child, rule_id, depth + 1, seen_ids, warnings)
   110→
   111→
   112→if __name__ == "__main__":
   113→    import pathlib
   114→
   115→    oscar_path = pathlib.Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "oscar.json"
   116→    with open(oscar_path) as f:
   117→        data = json.load(f)
   118→
   119→    is_valid, error = validate_structured_json(data)
   120→    if is_valid:
   121→        print("oscar.json is valid.")
   122→    else:
   123→        print(f"oscar.json is invalid: {error}")
   124→

1→"""
     2→B5: PDF Text Extractor
     3→
     4→Extracts text from downloaded PDFs using PyMuPDF (fitz).
     5→Preserves page structure and numbering for LLM consumption.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→from __future__ import annotations
     9→
    10→import logging
    11→import re
    12→from dataclasses import dataclass
    13→from pathlib import Path
    14→from typing import Optional
    15→
    16→import fitz  # PyMuPDF
    17→
    18→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    19→
    20→
    21→@dataclass
    22→class ExtractionResult:
    23→    text: str
    24→    page_count: int
    25→    error: Optional[str] = None
    26→
    27→
    28→def extract_text(pdf_path: str | Path) -> ExtractionResult:
    29→    """Extract text from a PDF file.
    30→
    31→    Returns full text with page markers, preserving numbered lists and hierarchy.
    32→    """
    33→    pdf_path = Path(pdf_path)
    34→    if not pdf_path.exists():
    35→        return ExtractionResult(text="", page_count=0, error=f"File not found: {pdf_path}")
    36→
    37→    try:
    38→        doc = fitz.open(str(pdf_path))
    39→        pages: list[str] = []
    40→
    41→        for page_num in range(len(doc)):
    42→            page = doc[page_num]
    43→            page_text = page.get_text("text", sort=True)
    44→
    45→            # Strip common headers/footers (page numbers, copyright lines)
    46→            lines = page_text.split("\n")
    47→            filtered = _strip_headers_footers(lines, page_num + 1, len(doc))
    48→            pages.append("\n".join(filtered))
    49→
    50→        doc.close()
    51→
    52→        full_text = "\n\n".join(
    53→            f"--- Page {i+1} ---\n{p}" for i, p in enumerate(pages) if p.strip()
    54→        )
    55→
    56→        return ExtractionResult(text=full_text, page_count=len(pages))
    57→
    58→    except Exception as e:
    59→        logger.error("Failed to extract text from %s: %s", pdf_path, e)
    60→        return ExtractionResult(text="", page_count=0, error=str(e))
    61→
    62→
    63→def _strip_headers_footers(lines: list[str], page_num: int, total_pages: int) -> list[str]:
    64→    """Remove common header/footer patterns from extracted lines."""
    65→    filtered = []
    66→    for line in lines:
    67→        stripped = line.strip()
    68→
    69→        # Skip empty lines at start/end (will be re-added by join)
    70→        if not stripped:
    71→            filtered.append("")
    72→            continue
    73→
    74→        # Skip standalone page numbers
    75→        if re.match(r"^\d+$", stripped):
    76→            continue
    77→        if re.match(r"^Page\s+\d+\s*(of\s+\d+)?$", stripped, re.I):
    78→            continue
    79→
    80→        # Skip "Confidential" / copyright footers
    81→        if re.match(r"^(confidential|©|\u00a9|all rights reserved)", stripped, re.I):
    82→            continue
    83→
    84→        filtered.append(line)
    85→
    86→    return filtered
    87→
    88→
    89→@dataclass
    90→class SectionResult:
    91→    text: str
    92→    confidence: float
    93→    logic: str
    94→
    95→
    96→# Patterns indicating the start of the initial medical necessity criteria
    97→_START_PATTERNS = [
    98→    re.compile(r"(?i)initial\s+(criteria|authorization|approval)"),
    99→    re.compile(r"(?i)criteria\s+for\s+medically\s+necessary"),
   100→    re.compile(r"(?i)medical\s+necessity\s+criteria"),
   101→    re.compile(r"(?i)conditions\s+for\s+coverage"),
   102→    re.compile(r"(?i)clinical\s+indications"),
   103→]
   104→
   105→# Patterns indicating the start of a non-initial section (stopping points)
   106→_END_PATTERNS = [
   107→    re.compile(r"(?i)continuation\s+(criteria|therapy|treatment|of\s+therapy)"),
   108→    re.compile(r"(?i)re-?authorization\s+criteria"),
   109→    re.compile(r"(?i)renewal\s+criteria"),
   110→    re.compile(r"(?i)repair[/,]\s*revision"),
   111→    re.compile(r"(?i)conversion\s+criteria"),
   112→    re.compile(r"(?i)removal\s+criteria"),
   113→    re.compile(r"(?i)experimental\s*[/&]\s*investigational"),
   114→    re.compile(r"(?i)relative\s+contraindications"),
   115→    re.compile(r"(?i)applicable\s+billing\s+codes"),
   116→    re.compile(r"(?i)HCPCS\s+(&|and)\s+CPT\s+codes"),
   117→    re.compile(r"(?i)procedures?\s*(&|and)\s*length\s+of\s+stay"),
   118→    re.compile(r"(?i)Medical Necessity Criteria for Reauthorization"),
   119→]
   120→
   121→
   122→def extract_initial_section(full_text: str) -> SectionResult:
   123→    """Extract only the 'Initial' criteria section from full PDF text.
   124→
   125→    Uses a line-by-line state machine approach with confidence scoring.
   126→    Returns SectionResult with text, confidence (0.0-1.0), and extraction logic.
   127→    """
   128→    lines = full_text.split("\n")
   129→    start_idx = -1
   130→    end_idx = -1
   131→    confidence = 0.0
   132→    logic = "No boundaries detected."
   133→    in_criteria = False
   134→
   135→    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
   136→        clean = line.strip()
   137→        if not clean:
   138→            continue
   139→
   140→        if not in_criteria:
   141→            for pattern in _START_PATTERNS:
   142→                if pattern.search(clean):
   143→                    start_idx = i
   144→                    in_criteria = True
   145→                    if "initial" in clean.lower():
   146→                        confidence = 0.95
   147→                        logic = f"Found explicit 'Initial' start at line {i}."
   148→                    else:
   149→                        confidence = 0.85
   150→                        logic = f"Found generic criteria start at line {i}."
   151→                    break
   152→        else:
   153→            for pattern in _END_PATTERNS:
   154→                if pattern.search(clean):
   155→                    end_idx = i
   156→                    logic += f" End marker '{clean[:40]}...' at line {i}."
   157→                    break
   158→
   159→            # Heuristic: uppercase block as section break
   160→            if end_idx == -1 and clean.isupper() and len(clean) > 10:
   161→                if any(kw in clean for kw in ("BACKGROUND", "SUMMARY", "REFERENCES")):
   162→                    end_idx = i
   163→                    logic += f" Uppercase block end at line {i}."
   164→
   165→            if end_idx != -1:
   166→                break
   167→
   168→    if start_idx == -1:
   169→        return SectionResult(
   170→            text=full_text,
   171→            confidence=0.30,
   172→            logic="Fallback: No start boundary found. Returning full text.",
   173→        )
   174→
   175→    if end_idx == -1:
   176→        end_idx = len(lines)
   177→        confidence -= 0.15
   178→        logic += " Reached EOF without explicit end marker."
   179→
   180→    section = "\n".join(lines[start_idx:end_idx])
   181→    logger.info(
   182→        "Extracted initial section: %d chars (confidence=%.2f)", len(section), confidence
   183→    )
   184→    return SectionResult(text=section, confidence=confidence, logic=logic)
   185→
   186→
   187→if __name__ == "__main__":
   188→    import sys
   189→    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)
   190→
   191→    if len(sys.argv) < 2:
   192→        print("Usage: python -m backend.extractor <pdf_path>")
   193→        sys.exit(1)
   194→
   195→    result = extract_text(sys.argv[1])
   196→    print(f"Pages: {result.page_count}")
   197→    print(f"Text length: {len(result.text)} chars")
   198→    if result.error:
   199→        print(f"Error: {result.error}")
   200→    else:
   201→        # Show first 500 chars
   202→        print(result.text[:500])
   203→        print("---")
   204→        # Try initial section extraction
   205→        section = extract_initial_section(result.text)
   206→        print(f"\nInitial section: {len(section.text)} chars (confidence={section.confidence:.2f})")
   207→        print(f"Logic: {section.logic}")
   208→        print(section.text[:500])
   209→

1→"""
     2→B6: LLM Structuring Pipeline
     3→
     4→Sends extracted PDF text to OpenAI GPT-4o and returns a structured JSON
     5→criteria tree matching the oscar.json format.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→from __future__ import annotations
     9→
    10→import json
    11→import logging
    12→import os
    13→from pathlib import Path
    14→from typing import Optional
    15→
    16→from openai import OpenAI
    17→from sqlalchemy import create_engine, event, text as sql_text
    18→
    19→from backend.extractor import ExtractionResult, SectionResult, extract_text, extract_initial_section
    20→from backend.validator import validate_structured_json
    21→
    22→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    23→
    24→OSCAR_JSON = json.loads((Path(__file__).parent.parent / "oscar.json").read_text())
    25→
    26→SYSTEM_PROMPT = """You are a medical policy criteria structurer. You convert medical guideline text into structured JSON decision trees.
    27→
    28→Your output MUST be valid JSON matching this exact schema:
    29→{
    30→  "title": "string - descriptive title of the criteria",
    31→  "insurance_name": "Oscar Health",
    32→  "rules": {
    33→    "rule_id": "string - hierarchical ID like 1, 1.1, 1.1.1",
    34→    "rule_text": "string - the criterion text",
    35→    "operator": "AND or OR - REQUIRED if this node has children",
    36→    "rules": [array of child nodes with same shape - REQUIRED if operator is present]
    37→  }
    38→}
    39→
    40→Rules:
    41→- The root "rules" object is a single node (not an array)
    42→- Leaf nodes have ONLY rule_id and rule_text (no operator, no rules array)
    43→- Non-leaf nodes MUST have both "operator" and "rules"
    44→- operator is "AND" when ALL criteria must be met (phrases like "all of the following", "and")
    45→- operator is "OR" when ANY criterion suffices (phrases like "one of the following", "or")
    46→- Use hierarchical rule_id numbering: 1, 1.1, 1.2, 1.2.1, etc.
    47→- Extract ONLY the INITIAL authorization/approval criteria, NOT reauthorization/continuation
    48→- Keep rule_text concise but complete
    49→
    50→Here is an example of correct output:
    51→""" + json.dumps(OSCAR_JSON, indent=2)
    52→
    53→USER_PROMPT_TEMPLATE = """Extract the INITIAL medical necessity criteria from this Oscar Health clinical guideline and structure them as a JSON decision tree.
    54→
    55→IMPORTANT:
    56→- Extract ONLY the initial criteria (NOT reauthorization, continuation, repair, or revision criteria)
    57→- If there are multiple criteria sections, extract only the first/initial one
    58→- Preserve the AND/OR logic exactly as stated in the document
    59→
    60→Guideline text:
    61→---
    62→{text}
    63→---
    64→
    65→Return ONLY the JSON object, no markdown formatting, no explanation."""
    66→
    67→
    68→def _parse_and_repair(raw: str) -> dict:
    69→    """Parse LLM output, repairing malformed JSON if needed."""
    70→    try:
    71→        return json.loads(raw)
    72→    except json.JSONDecodeError:
    73→        # Strip markdown fencing
    74→        cleaned = raw.strip()
    75→        if cleaned.startswith("```json"):
    76→            cleaned = cleaned[7:]
    77→        if cleaned.startswith("```"):
    78→            cleaned = cleaned[3:]
    79→        if cleaned.endswith("```"):
    80→            cleaned = cleaned[:-3]
    81→        cleaned = cleaned.strip()
    82→
    83→        # Try again after stripping
    84→        try:
    85→            return json.loads(cleaned)
    86→        except json.JSONDecodeError:
    87→            pass
    88→
    89→        # Try fast-json-repair if available
    90→        try:
    91→            from fast_json_repair import repair_json
    92→            repaired = repair_json(cleaned)
    93→            return json.loads(repaired)
    94→        except (ImportError, Exception):
    95→            pass
    96→
    97→        # Try json_repair as fallback
    98→        try:
    99→            import json_repair
   100→            return json_repair.loads(cleaned)
   101→        except (ImportError, Exception):
   102→            pass
   103→
   104→        raise ValueError(f"Irreparable JSON: {raw[:200]}...")
   105→
   106→
   107→def _clean_leaf_nodes(node: dict) -> None:
   108→    """Remove hallucinated operators from leaf nodes."""
   109→    if "rules" not in node or not node.get("rules"):
   110→        node.pop("operator", None)
   111→        node.pop("rules", None)
   112→    else:
   113→        for child in node["rules"]:
   114→            _clean_leaf_nodes(child)
   115→
   116→
   117→def structure_text(text: str, title_hint: str = "", max_attempts: int = 3) -> dict:
   118→    """Send text to OpenAI and get structured JSON back.
   119→
   120→    Includes semantic retry: on validation failure, feeds error back to LLM.
   121→    """
   122→    client = OpenAI(api_key=os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"))
   123→
   124→    user_prompt = USER_PROMPT_TEMPLATE.format(text=text[:12000])  # Token limit safety
   125→
   126→    for attempt in range(max_attempts):
   127→        response = client.chat.completions.create(
   128→            model="gpt-4o",
   129→            messages=[
   130→                {"role": "system", "content": SYSTEM_PROMPT},
   131→                {"role": "user", "content": user_prompt},
   132→            ],
   133→            response_format={"type": "json_object"},
   134→            temperature=0.1,
   135→            max_tokens=4096,
   136→        )
   137→
   138→        content = response.choices[0].message.content
   139→        result = _parse_and_repair(content)
   140→
   141→        # Clean leaf nodes
   142→        if "rules" in result:
   143→            _clean_leaf_nodes(result["rules"])
   144→
   145→        # Validate
   146→        is_valid, error = validate_structured_json(result)
   147→        if is_valid and (error is None or error.startswith("Warnings:")):
   148→            logger.info("LLM returned valid JSON (attempt %d): %s", attempt + 1, result.get("title", "?"))
   149→            return result
   150→
   151→        # Semantic retry: feed error back
   152→        logger.warning("Attempt %d validation failed: %s", attempt + 1, error)
   153→        if "continuation" in (error or "").lower():
   154→            user_prompt += "\n\nCRITICAL ERROR: You extracted Continuation criteria. ONLY extract the INITIAL criteria."
   155→        else:
   156→            user_prompt += f"\n\nERROR IN PREVIOUS OUTPUT: {error}\nPlease fix the JSON structure."
   157→
   158→    # Return last result even if imperfect
   159→    logger.warning("All %d attempts exhausted, returning last result", max_attempts)
   160→    return result
   161→
   162→
   163→def _make_engine():
   164→    db_url = os.getenv("DATABASE_URL", "sqlite+pysqlite:///./data/app.db")
   165→    engine = create_engine(
   166→        db_url,
   167→        connect_args={"check_same_thread": False} if db_url.startswith("sqlite") else {},
   168→        pool_pre_ping=True,
   169→    )
   170→    if db_url.startswith("sqlite"):
   171→        @event.listens_for(engine, "connect")
   172→        def _pragmas(dbapi_conn, _):
   173→            cur = dbapi_conn.cursor()
   174→            cur.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON;")
   175→            cur.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL;")
   176→            cur.execute("PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000;")
   177→            cur.close()
   178→    return engine
   179→
   180→
   181→UPSERT_STRUCTURED_SQL = """
   182→INSERT INTO structured_policies (policy_id, extracted_text, structured_json, llm_model, llm_prompt, validation_error)
   183→VALUES (:policy_id, :extracted_text, :structured_json, :llm_model, :llm_prompt, :validation_error)
   184→ON CONFLICT(policy_id) DO UPDATE SET
   185→    extracted_text = excluded.extracted_text,
   186→    structured_json = excluded.structured_json,
   187→    structured_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
   188→    llm_model = excluded.llm_model,
   189→    llm_prompt = excluded.llm_prompt,
   190→    validation_error = excluded.validation_error
   191→"""
   192→
   193→
   194→def run_structuring(limit: int = 10) -> dict:
   195→    """Structure at least `limit` downloaded policies.
   196→
   197→    Picks policies that have successful downloads but no structured_policies entry yet.
   198→    """
   199→    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(levelname)s %(message)s")
   200→
   201→    from backend.migrate import run_migrations
   202→    run_migrations()
   203→
   204→    engine = _make_engine()
   205→
   206→    # Find policies with successful downloads but no structuring yet
   207→    with engine.begin() as conn:
   208→        rows = conn.execute(sql_text("""
   209→            SELECT p.id, p.title, d.stored_location
   210→            FROM policies p
   211→            JOIN downloads d ON d.policy_id = p.id AND d.error IS NULL AND d.stored_location != ''
   212→            LEFT JOIN structured_policies sp ON sp.policy_id = p.id
   213→            WHERE sp.id IS NULL
   214→            ORDER BY p.id
   215→            LIMIT :limit
   216→        """), {"limit": limit}).mappings().all()
   217→
   218→    if not rows:
   219→        logger.info("No policies to structure (all done or no downloads)")
   220→        return {"total": 0, "success": 0, "failed": 0}
   221→
   222→    stats = {"total": len(rows), "success": 0, "failed": 0}
   223→
   224→    for row in rows:
   225→        policy_id = row["id"]
   226→        title = row["title"]
   227→        pdf_path = row["stored_location"]
   228→
   229→        logger.info("Structuring: %s (id=%d)", title, policy_id)
   230→
   231→        try:
   232→            # Extract text
   233→            extraction = extract_text(pdf_path)
   234→            if extraction.error:
   235→                raise RuntimeError(f"Text extraction failed: {extraction.error}")
   236→            section = extract_initial_section(extraction.text)
   237→            initial_text = section.text
   238→
   239→            # Structure with LLM
   240→            result = structure_text(initial_text, title_hint=title)
   241→
   242→            # Validate
   243→            is_valid, error = validate_structured_json(result)
   244→            validation_error = error if not is_valid else None
   245→
   246→            if validation_error:
   247→                logger.warning("Validation issue for %s: %s", title, validation_error)
   248→
   249→            # Store
   250→            with engine.begin() as conn:
   251→                conn.execute(sql_text(UPSERT_STRUCTURED_SQL), {
   252→                    "policy_id": policy_id,
   253→                    "extracted_text": initial_text[:50000],  # Truncate for storage
   254→                    "structured_json": json.dumps(result),
   255→                    "llm_model": "gpt-4o",
   256→                    "llm_prompt": "v1-system+user-initial-only",
   257→                    "validation_error": validation_error,
   258→                })
   259→
   260→            stats["success"] += 1
   261→            logger.info("  OK: %s", title)
   262→
   263→        except Exception as e:
   264→            logger.error("  FAILED: %s — %s", title, e)
   265→            # Store the failure
   266→            try:
   267→                with engine.begin() as conn:
   268→                    conn.execute(sql_text(UPSERT_STRUCTURED_SQL), {
   269→                        "policy_id": policy_id,
   270→                        "extracted_text": "",
   271→                        "structured_json": "{}",
   272→                        "llm_model": "gpt-4o",
   273→                        "llm_prompt": "v1-system+user-initial-only",
   274→                        "validation_error": str(e),
   275→                    })
   276→            except Exception:
   277→                pass
   278→            stats["failed"] += 1
   279→
   280→    logger.info("Structuring complete: %s", stats)
   281→    return stats
   282→
   283→
   284→if __name__ == "__main__":
   285→    from dotenv import load_dotenv
   286→    load_dotenv()
   287→
   288→    import sys
   289→    limit = int(sys.argv[1]) if len(sys.argv) > 1 else 10
   290→    stats = run_structuring(limit=limit)
   291→    print(f"\nDone! {stats}")
   292→

# Research Prompts for Oscar Medical Guidelines Project

These prompts are designed to be used with ChatGPT Deep Research and Gemini Deep Research to gather everything needed to implement the full project. Each prompt is self-contained with all context embedded.
